title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School
excerpt: NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined school leadership in Monterey, California, to commission the system, which will fuel AI education and research at the US military’s premier technical graduate university.
content_html:
NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang today visited the Naval Postgraduate School in Monterey, California, to commission an NVIDIA DGX GB300 system — bringing one of the world’s most powerful AI platforms fully online for the students, researchers and faculty at the U.S. military’s flagship graduate university.

“Our nation depends on our men and women who fight on the front lines. Nothing is more valuable to you than information and insight, and information and insight in a timely way, and to understand its impact and consequence,” Huang said at the event. “I can’t imagine anything more important.”

The DGX GB300 supercomputer with NVIDIA Mission Control software gives NPS’s more than 1,500 in-resident students and 600 faculty on-premises access to large-scale AI computing, including model training and inference capability for applications spanning weather prediction, cybersecurity, and disaster resilience and response planning.

The commissioning, which took place during the school’s three-day Converge @ NPS event, marks the latest chapter in an ongoing collaboration to develop AI-based technologies at NPS for education and real-world applications. 

It’s built based on an NVIDIA AI Technology Center on the Monterey campus — a dedicated hub for AI research and graduate instruction, now anchored by the DGX GB300.

“As we modernize our technology, we must also modernize how we educate our leaders,” said Admiral Samuel Paparo, commander of the U.S. Pacific Command. “Access to advanced computing capability means NPS students and faculty understand the opportunities and responsibilities that come with these technologies.”

Training Leaders to Build With AI

NPS educates active-duty officers and international partners across disciplines — from space operations to ocean science — awarding graduate degrees while centering each program on applied research with direct relevance to real-world problems. 

“Leadership is about being in service of something else. You’re in service of your team. You’re in service of your country. You’re in service of your mission,” Huang said to attendees. “And all of this technology is just tools to help you.” 

The DGX GB300 gives those programs new technical depth: the ability to train foundation models in house, run high-fidelity simulations at scale and develop AI tools with immediate applicability.

Huang told attendees that even if they aren’t experts in computer science, today’s computers running AI make it easy. 

“The most important advice that I would give to someone is to engage the technology,” Huang said. “It’s not as hard as you think it is, and the reason for that is because, on first principles, technology is supposed to get smarter and smarter over time.” 

NVIDIA has also expanded the collaboration through its Deep Learning Institute, putting instructor toolkits in the hands of NPS faculty so AI is woven through graduate curricula across departments. 

“Many of you will command in AI-enabled environments. Information will move at lightning speeds, compressing response times,” Paparo said. “Our advantage will come from leaders, like you, who can use the technology to see, understand, decide and act faster while exercising the judgment, experience and leadership that machines cannot provide. That education is happening here at NPS.”

From Ocean Models to Digital Twins

NPS runs a regular cadence of hackathons that have produced advances in autonomy, ocean research and operations planning — applied work that demands serious computing to scale. 

Researchers are using AI to model sea conditions, predict atmospheric changes and build digital twins of complex environments — work that feeds directly into the scientific domains where NPS has deep expertise.

The DGX GB300 will now power these workloads on campus. 

Through the school’s partnership with MITRE, a nonprofit organization, NPS has developed a framework built on NVIDIA Omniverse libraries that provides high-fidelity digital twin environments for simulating real-world navigation and decision-making under uncertain conditions.

To learn more, watch NPS and NVIDIA researchers present on AI modeling and simulation at NVIDIA GTC Washington, D.C.

NVIDIA Partner Ecosystem Brings AI Infrastructure to Life

The DGX GB300 at NPS didn’t come together in isolation. DDN, VAST and Vertiv joined the effort as hardware and systems integration partners. 

DDN contributed high-performance data infrastructure to help researchers efficiently access, manage, protect and scale data for demanding AI workloads. 

VAST Data provided a unified data platform to enable secure data access and management across edge, core and cloud environments. 

Vertiv provided the racks, cooling and power infrastructure needed to bring the system online, as well as installation sequencing, testing and commissioning support, and fluid management and monitoring. 

Learn more about NVIDIA’s collaboration with NPS and explore the NVIDIA DGX GB300.

sentences.en: VAST Data provided a unified data platform to enable secure data access and management across edge, core and cloud environments.

blocks.html_en: Vertiv provided the racks, cooling and power infrastructure needed to bring the system online, as well as installation sequencing, testing and commissioning support, and fluid management and monitoring.

sentences.zh: Vertiv 提供系统上线所需的机架、冷却和电力基础设施，以及安装排序、测试和调试支持以及流体管理和监控。

blocks.html_en: Learn more about NVIDIA’s collaboration with NPS and explore the NVIDIA DGX GB300.

sentences.en: Learn more about NVIDIA’s collaboration with NPS and explore the NVIDIA DGX GB300.
sentences.zh: 了解有关 NVIDIA 与 NPS 合作的更多信息并探索 NVIDIA DGX GB300。

content_html_zh:
NVIDIA 创始人兼首席执行官黄仁勋今天访问了位于加利福尼亚州蒙特雷的海军研究生院，委托使用 NVIDIA DGX GB300 系统，为美国军方旗舰研究生大学的学生、研究人员和教职人员带来世界上最强大的 AI 平台之一的全面在线服务。

“我们的国家依赖于奋战在前线的男男女女。 对你来说，没有什么比信息和洞察力、及时的信息和洞察力，以及了解其影响和后果更有价值的了。”黄在活动中说道。 “我无法想象还有什么比这更重要的事情了。”

配备 NVIDIA Mission Control 软件的 DGX GB300 超级计算机使 NPS 的 1,500 多名驻校学生和 600 名本地教职人员能够访问大规模 AI 计算，包括针对天气预报、网络安全以及灾难恢复和响应规划等应用的模型训练和推理能力。

这次调试是在学校为期三天的 Converge @ NPS 活动期间进行的，标志着 NPS 为教育和实际应用开发基于人工智能的技术的持续合作的最新篇章。

它基于蒙特利校区的 NVIDIA AI 技术中心构建，这是一个专门的 AI 研究和研究生指导中心，现在以 DGX GB300 为基础。

美国太平洋司令部司令塞缪尔·帕帕罗海军上将表示：“在我们实现技术现代化的同时，我们还必须实现领导者教育方式的现代化。” “获得先进的计算能力意味着 NPS 的学生和教师了解这些技术带来的机会和责任。”

培训领导者使用人工智能进行构建

NPS 为现役军官和国际合作伙伴提供跨学科教育——从太空作战到海洋科学——授予研究生学位，同时将每个项目的重点放在与现实世界问题直接相关的应用研究上。

“领导力就是为其他事情服务。 你为你的团队服务。 你正在为你的国家服务。 你们正在为自己的使命服务，”黄对与会者说。 “所有这些技术都只是帮助你的工具。”

DGX GB300 为这些程序提供了新的技术深度：能够在内部训练基础模型、大规模运行高保真模拟以及开发可立即应用的 AI 工具。

黄告诉与会者，即使他们不是计算机科学专家，今天运行人工智能的计算机也能让这一切变得容易。

“我给某人的最重要的建议是利用技术，”黄说。 “这并不像你想象的那么难，其原因是因为，根据首要原则，随着时间的推移，技术应该变得越来越聪明。”

NVIDIA 还通过其深度学习学院扩大了合作，将讲师工具包交给 NPS 教师，以便将人工智能融入到跨院系的研究生课程中。

“你们中的许多人将在人工智能支持的环境中发号施令。 信息将以闪电般的速度传播，从而压缩响应时间，”帕帕罗说。 “我们的优势将来自于像你们这样的领导者，他们可以利用技术更快地观察、理解、决策和行动，同时运用机器无法提供的判断、经验和领导力。 这种教育正在 NPS 进行。”

从海洋模型到数字孪生

NPS 定期举办黑客马拉松，在自主性、海洋研究和运营规划等需要严格计算才能扩展的应用工作方面取得了进展。

研究人员正在使用人工智能来模拟海洋条件、预测大气变化并构建复杂环境的数字孪生——这些工作直接进入 NPS 拥有深厚专业知识的科学领域。

DGX GB300 现在将为校园内的这些工作负载提供支持。

通过学校与非营利组织 MITRE 的合作，NPS 开发了一个基于 NVIDIA Omniverse 库的框架，该框架提供高保真数字孪生环境，用于在不确定条件下模拟现实世界的导航和决策。

要了解更多信息，请观看 NPS 和 NVIDIA 研究人员在华盛顿特区 NVIDIA GTC 上展示的 AI 建模和模拟。

NVIDIA 合作伙伴生态系统将 AI 基础设施带入生活

NPS 的 DGX GB300 并不是孤立的。 DDN、VAST 和 Vertiv 作为硬件和系统集成合作伙伴加入了这一努力。

DDN 提供了高性能数据基础设施，帮助研究人员高效访问、管理、保护和扩展数据，以满足要求苛刻的人工智能工作负载。

VAST Data提供了一个统一的数据平台，以实现跨边缘、核心和云环境的安全数据访问和管理。

Vertiv 提供系统上线所需的机架、冷却和电力基础设施，以及安装排序、测试和调试支持以及流体管理和监控。

了解有关 NVIDIA 与 NPS 合作的更多信息并探索 NVIDIA DGX GB300。
